summary: Verified both targeted bug fixes on ported Scindia Household backend. Bug Fix 1 (GET /api/users KeyError 'name') and Bug Fix 2 (openapi/docs/redoc under /api prefix) are both resolved. Seed logins and auth/me regression checks pass.
passed: GET /api/openapi.json -> HTTP 200, info.title='Scindia Household API v2', 24 paths
POST /api/auth/login for admin/manager/tasker returns correct role & name
GET /api/auth/me for all 3 tokens returns correct role & name

Tolerant _to_user_public: inserted raw user doc with NO 'name' field (email noname@example.com) via mongosh, GET /api/users returned HTTP 200 with count=4 and the nameless doc surfaced with name falling back to email ('noname@example.com')
notes: During fallback verification, an initial attempt with email 'noname@scindia.test' returned HTTP 500 due to Pydantic EmailStr rejecting the reserved '.test' TLD (not a fix regression). Retest with 'noname@example.com' confirmed the fallback works. Backfill update_many at startup does not overwrite docs inserted AFTER startup, so the runtime tolerance in _to_user_public is what protects the endpoint — verified working.
